{{Short description="Lightest quark flavour; constituent of protons and neutrons}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Up quark
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Quark]], [[Elementary particle]], [[Proton]], [[Neutron]], [[Standard Model]]
| composition = [[Elementary particle]]
| statistics = [[Fermion]]
| symbol = u
| generation = First
| charge = +2/3 e
| mass = 2.2 ± 0.5 MeV/c²
| spin = 1/2
}}

The '''up quark''' (symbol: <math>u</math>) is the lightest of all [[quark]]s, a type of [[elementary particle]], and a major constituent of ordinary [[matter]]. Along with the [[down quark]], it forms the first generation of quarks in the [[Standard Model]].

== Role in matter ==
The [[proton]] (<math>uud</math>) contains two up quarks and one down quark:
* Charge: <math>+2/3 + 2/3 - 1/3 = +1</math>

The [[neutron]] (<math>udd</math>) contains one up quark and two down quarks:
* Charge: <math>+2/3 - 1/3 - 1/3 = 0</math>

Together, the up and down quarks account for nearly all visible mass in the [[universe]].

== Properties ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Property !! Value
|-
| Mass || 2.2 ± 0.5 MeV/c² (~0.2% of proton mass)
|-
| Charge || +2/3 e
|-
| [[Spin (physics)|Spin]] || 1/2
|-
| [[Isospin]] (<math>I_3</math>) || +1/2
|-
| [[Colour charge]] || Red, green, or blue
|}

The up quark is the second-lightest quark after the [[down quark]]. The proton's mass (~938 MeV/c²) arises primarily from the [[binding energy]] of QCD, not from the rest masses of its constituent quarks (~9 MeV/c² total).

== Weak interactions ==
The up quark participates in [[weak interaction]]s, coupling to the down quark via the [[Cabibbo–Kobayashi–Maskawa matrix|CKM matrix]]:

:<math>\begin{pmatrix} d' \\ s' \\ b' \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix}
V_{ud} & V_{us} & V_{ub} \\
V_{cd} & V_{cs} & V_{cb} \\
V_{td} & V_{ts} & V_{tb}
\end{pmatrix} \begin{pmatrix} d \\ s \\ b \end{pmatrix}</math>

The dominant coupling is <math>u \leftrightarrow d</math> with <math>|V_{ud}| \approx 0.974</math>.

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Down quark]]
* [[Elementary particle]]
* [[Neutron]]
* [[Proton]]
* [[Quark]]
* [[Standard Model]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Quarks]]
[[Category:Elementary particles]]
[[Category:Particle physics]]
